name: holospaces egress
description: Local egress for holospaces guests: opens raw TCP for your own holospace's network via the Direct Sockets API, so a Chromebook's devcontainer reaches the internet with no node and no proxy.
_comment_minimal: Requested permissions are kept to the MINIMUM for a fast Chrome Web Store review. The egress role connects to an IP:port the guest's NAT already chose and forwards opaque bytes — it never calls fetch(), reads a tab, injects a content script, or stores data. So this extension requests NO host_permissions, NO tabs, NO storage, NO scripting, NO webRequest. Its only capability is raw sockets (Direct Sockets), and its only inbound surface is a message port from the operator's own holospaces origin.
_comment_direct_sockets: Direct Sockets (TCPSocket/UDPSocket) is a powerful, gated capability. It is enabled OUT OF BAND — for an enterprise/managed device via the DirectSocketsAllowedForUrls policy, or for development via chrome://flags — NOT via a broad store permission, which keeps the store-facing permission surface minimal. Confirm the exact gating for your Chrome version/channel.
_comment_connect: Only the operator's own holospaces origin may message this extension — the egress is the operator's, self-sovereign, not open to any page. Trim/extend `matches` to exactly your deployment's origin(s); narrower is faster to review.
